title: Register for Youth Achieving Results (Visual Arts)
description: Register a young visual artist for the Youth Achieving Results visual arts programme.
summary: Youth Achieving Results (YAR) supports young Barbadians in the arts. The Visual Arts strand is for people working in painting, drawing, photography, sculpture and digital art.
You get classes with experienced practitioners, mentoring from established artists, and a chance to show your work at end-of-programme exhibitions.
The programme is open to people aged 12 to 30. If you are under 16, a parent or guardian needs to register you.
There is no fee. You can include a portfolio or sample of recent work with your application if you have one – it helps the team understand where you are and how the programme can support you.
who_can_apply: parents or guardians registering a child under 16

items: the participant’s full name, address and date of birth
the visual art they want to focus on (painting, drawing, photography, sculpture, digital art)
for under-16s, the parent or guardian’s details

items.name: Portfolio or sample of recent work

how_long: Places are usually confirmed within 2 weeks of the closing date.

options.heading: Online
content: Complete the Youth Achieving Results (Visual Arts): Registration Form on the Ministry website.

contact.agency: Ministry of Youth, Sports and Community Empowerment
contact.address:
Sky Mall, Haggatt Hall
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Aged 12 to 30’ is the commonly-quoted eligibility range.
‘Places are usually confirmed within 2 weeks’ is an estimate.
